background: Exercise 18.5 examined responses to treatment in 196 leprosy patients. Descriptive statistics suggested patients in the high skin-infiltration group were more likely to demonstrate a favorable response. Table 18.13 has taken the 2-by-5 cross tabulation from Table 18.10 and transposed it into a 5-by-2 table. It also calculates odds and odds ratios, which become progressively smaller as we proceed down the table.

task.query: We now ask whether this trend is statistically significant.
task.answer: A. Hypotheses. $H_0$ : no linear trend in improvement in the population versus $H_a$ : linear trend in the population. B. Test statistic. $$ \begin{aligned} O= & \sum a_i s_i=(7)(1)+(15)(2)+\ldots+(1)(5)=142 \\ E= & \frac{m_i}{N} \sum n_i s_i=\frac{52}{196}((18)(1)+(42)(2)+\ldots+(12)(5))= \\ 159.184 & \\ V= & \frac{m_1 m_2}{N^2(N-1)}\left[N \sum n_i s_i^2-\left(\sum n_i s_i\right)^2\right] \\ = & \frac{(52)(144)}{196^2(196-1)}\left[196\left((18)\left(1^2\right)+(42)\left(2^2\right)+\ldots+(12)\left(5^2\right)\right)-\right. \\ & \left.((18)(1)+(42)(2)+\ldots+(12)(5))^2\right]=44.525 \end{aligned} $$ $$ z_{\ ext {stat,trend }}=\frac{O-E}{\sqrt{V}}=\frac{142-159.184}{\sqrt{44.525}}=-2.58 $$ C. $\boldsymbol{P}$-value. The two-sided $P=0.00988$, providing strong evidence against the null hypothesis.

meta info.caption: Test for trend in response, ordinal response variable (Leprosy treatment).
